description: Demonstrates social signal rejection — a viral post claims a gas leak but no official alert exists. Shows the system's signal validation.

properties.headline: Air Quality Alert for San Francisco Bay Area
properties.areaDesc: San Francisco; Oakland; Berkeley; Marin County
properties.description: An Air Quality Alert is in effect due to elevated particulate matter from offshore winds. Sensitive groups including those with asthma or heart conditions should reduce outdoor activity.
properties.instruction: Limit prolonged or heavy outdoor exertion. Keep windows closed. Sensitive groups should stay indoors.

social_signals.text: HUGE gas explosion near Market Street SF!! Can smell gas everywhere, evacuate NOW!!
social_signals.note: High virality but low-reliability source and no official corroboration — capped at inform, not surfaced as warning

demo_voice_prompt: I saw something on social media about a gas explosion near Market Street. Is that real?
expected_agent_response: I found one official alert in your area — an Air Quality Advisory for the Bay Area today due to offshore winds. There's no official report of a gas explosion near Market Street. The social media post hasn't been confirmed by any official source. I'd recommend monitoring official channels. Shall I check for any updates?
key_demo_point: Haven surfaces only the official Air Quality Alert at 'inform' severity. It does not amplify the unverified social signal to a higher tier.
